Home / Services / Professional Tree Installation

Professional Tree Installation

Right tree, right place - tailored to Charlotte soils.

The failed red maple in your front yard? Probably planted too deep in clay without proper amendments. Our planting protocol starts with soil analysis - we identify ideal native species for Charlotte's challenging conditions. In sandy Gastonia soils, that might mean drought-resistant post oaks. For compacted urban lots in NoDa, columnar varieties that won't interfere with foundations.

We go beyond basic ball-and-burlap installation with root washing techniques that prevent circling roots - a leading cause of premature decline. Each planting includes mycorrhizal inoculation to help roots penetrate our dense clay, plus a 2-year guarantee covering replacement if the tree fails to establish.

New construction clients in Fort Mill receive complete tree protection plans meeting municipal landscape requirements. We'll stake trees properly (never with wire in bark) and provide detailed watering guides accounting for Charlotte's variable rainfall.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Site evaluation including underground utility clearance
  2. 02 Step 2: Custom soil amendment blend preparation
  3. 03 Step 3: Bare-root preparation or root ball inspection
  4. 04 Step 4: Structural pruning at time of planting

Benefits

  • 2-year establishment guarantee
  • Free soil test with every planting
  • Native species recommendations
  • Post-planting care instructions

Frequently Asked Questions

What trees thrive best in Charlotte?

Drought-tolerant natives like willow oak and American hornbeam outperform imported species. We avoid problematic trees like Bradford pears that split in ice storms.

How often should new trees be watered?

Deep watering 2-3 times weekly for first six months - more during Matthews' dry autumns. We provide soil moisture meters to prevent overwatering in clay.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with professional tree installation? Contact us today.
